title: Install the WRD in the Application Server
breadcrumbs: Installing and Configuring Uniface on iSeries
Configuring for Web Deployment on iSeries
Install the WRD in the Application Server
text: Install the WRD in the Application Server You can now deploy the WAR file ( wrd.war ) you created earlier. See Configure the WRD and Create a wrd.war File on iSeries . Transfer the wrd.war file to the iSeries IFS. If the wrd.war file is not located on the iSeries IFS, transfer it to an IFS directory now, using binary FTP, or drag and drop in the Windows Explorer, to a shared IFS directory. On the IBM Web Administration for i page, select the Manage tab. There are four additional tabs under the top tabs. In the Application Servers tab, select the newly-created application server in Server . On the left pane, under Application Server Wizards , click Install New Application . On the right side, the Install New Application page appears. It has only one edit box and a browse button. Enter or browse for the fully qualified, absolute path to the wrd.war file in the IFS. Then click Next . Now you are asked for the application name , which is wrd , and for the Context root . This is the part of the URL that immediately follows the machine name and port number. For example, if you want the Uniface WRD to be accessed with a URL that has the following form: http:// iSeriesNetworkName :10000/uniface/ then /uniface would be the context root. When finished click Next . On the next screen accept the defaults by clicking Next . Now you will see a summary of what you filled in. Click Finish to start the installation of the wrd.war file. A new page is displayed, with a list of all applications currently installed in the application server. The wrd is among them, but in front of it is a yellow bullet with an hourglass symbol, indicating that its installation is in progress. Again, depending on your machine type and its workload, this might take some time to complete. When it has completed, the yellow bullet changes to red, indicating that it is installed but not running, or to green, indicating that it is installed and running. Was this topic helpful?
